data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T092937-17ca1484/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T092937-17ca1484/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T092937-17ca1484/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T092937-17ca1484/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T092937-17ca1484/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-092c4343">
    <title>work: keep live worker state out of tracked DDx files</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch` currently dirties tracked files while a worker is merely alive. Operators then see `.ddx/beads.jsonl` and `.ddx/run-state*` changes even when no durable bead outcome has happened, and concurrent watchers can race the same moving state.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go:869-925` `ClaimWithOptions` persists live claim state (`status=in_progress`, `owner`, `claimed-at`, `claimed-pid`, `claimed-machine`, `claimed-session`, `claimed-worktree`, `work-heartbeat-at`) into `.ddx/beads.jsonl` before touching the external lease.
- `cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go:15-24` and `cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go:64-75` already write an external lease, but the lease only carries `bead_id`, `updated_at`, and `pid`, so operator-facing claim metadata still depends on tracker mutation.
- `cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:100-125` writes live attempt state under `.ddx/run-state/` and `.ddx/run-state.json`; `cli/cmd/init.go:18-31` ignores these paths for new repos, but existing tracked run-state files still produce dirty deletions when cleanup runs.
- `cli/internal/agent/worker_liveness_sidecar_test.go:22-28` currently documents the tracker as the durable claim marker; that contract now conflicts with the desired live-state policy.

PROPOSED FIX
Move worker-owned live claim state fully into sidecar state. Extend the claim lease record to carry owner/session/worktree/machine/started_at/pid/updated_at and make the `ddx work` / `ddx try` worker claim path update only that lease until a terminal durable outcome occurs. `ReadyExecution` must exclude beads with a fresh sidecar lease and include beads whose sidecar lease is absent or stale. Manual tracker lifecycle operations can remain explicit tracker operations, but ordinary worker liveness must not dirty `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.

Also migrate run-state policy so live run-state files are never tracked: add a migration/cleanup path that removes tracked `.ddx/run-state.json` and `.ddx/run-state/*.json` from the git index, keeps the ignore rules, and proves cleanup does not create tracked deletions.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change bead close/proposed/blocked/cancelled semantics.
- Do not drop execution evidence or attempt metrics.
- Do not remove operator visibility; surface live owner/bead/attempt from sidecars instead of the tracker.
- Do not manually edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` in tests or implementation.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead -run 'TestWorkerClaimLeaseDoesNotMutateTracker|TestReadyExecutionExcludesFreshSidecarLease|TestReadyExecutionReclaimsStaleSidecarLease' -count=1` passes.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestWorkLoop_ClaimAndHeartbeatDoNotMutateTrackerBeforeOutcome|TestWorkerStatusSidecar_RecordsCurrentBeadAttemptAndLastActivity' -count=1` passes and asserts `.ddx/beads.jsonl` bytes are unchanged from before worker claim through pre-outcome heartbeat ticks.
3. `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestCleanupRunStateDoesNotDirtyTrackedRepo|TestInitGitignoreRunStateMigration' -count=1` passes and proves tracked legacy `.ddx/run-state.json` / `.ddx/run-state/*.json` are removed from the index or otherwise cleaned without leaving git status dirty.
4. `rg -n 'work-heartbeat-at|claimed-at|claimed-pid|claimed-machine|claimed-session|claimed-worktree' cli/internal/bead cli/internal/agent` shows no production worker-live-state write to `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; compatibility reads for stale legacy rows are allowed only when documented in comments and tests.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:agent, area:execution, area:bead, kind:bug, operator-ux, dirty-tree</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-c191e91a" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="b73f6eb916fcfb0cc3a4bb4ade4d91d674681835" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260515T092937-17ca1484"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Committed as `34cea78f8` with message `fix: keep worker live state out of tracked ddx files [ddx-092c4343]`.

Worker live claim state now stays in the sidecar lease instead of `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `ReadyExecution` excludes fresh leases and reclaims stale ones, `bead show` surfaces live claim metadata from the lease, and `init` now untracks legacy `.ddx/run-state.json` / `.ddx/run-state/` entries so cleanup does not leave tracked run-state deletions.

Verified with:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead -run 'TestWorkerClaimLeaseDoesNotMutateTracker|TestReadyExecutionExcludesFreshSidecarLease|TestReadyExecutionReclaimsStaleSidecarLease' -count=1`
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestWorkLoop_ClaimAndHeartbeatDoNotMutateTrackerBeforeOutcome|TestWorkerStatusSidecar_RecordsCurrentBeadAttemptAndLastActivity' -count=1`
- `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestCleanupRunStateDoesNotDirtyTrackedRepo|TestInitGitignoreRunStateMigration' -count=1`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`
